Our team specializes in maximizing private insurance benefits to ensure your clinical stay at our Des Moines drug rehab is fully supported. Call us today to verify your benefits. 100% free.

sections.heading: How to Spot Drug Addiction: The Signs
sections.subheading: Early warning signs that substance use may be turning into addiction
body: Drug addiction doesn’t always look obvious at first. In many cases, it develops gradually through changes in behavior and daily functioning. Symptoms can vary depending on the substance used and the individual, but there are common patterns that often appear across most types of addiction. Common signs of drug addiction include:

items.title: Using drugs more often or in higher amounts
Hiding or lying about substance use
Loss of control despite negative consequences
Withdrawing from family, friends, or activities
Changes in sleep patterns or energy levels

body: According to the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration , 48.4 million people in the U.S. had a substance use disorder in 2024 (about 1 in 6 Americans) highlighting how common and often overlooked addiction can be. If you recognize these signs in yourself or someone close to you, seeking professional support early can prevent addiction from worsening and make recovery more achievable.

sections.heading: Medical Drug Detox In Des Moines: Manage Withdrawal Safely
sections.subheading: Stabilizing your body before starting the work of long-term recovery
body: When someone stops using drugs after prolonged use, the body goes through withdrawal , a process where it reacts to the absence of the substance it has become dependent on. Withdrawal symptoms can range from uncomfortable to severe, depending on the substance, duration of use, and individual health. Common symptoms may include:

items.title: In more serious cases: seizures, heart complications, or severe dehydration

sections.heading: Managing Drug Withdrawal Symptoms Safely:

items.text: Preparing your body and mind to transition into the next level of treatment

sections.heading: What Drug Rehab in Des Moines Looks Like
body: After detox, recovery focuses on understanding addiction, changing behaviors, and building long-term stability. At Des Moines Wellness Center, we offer every drug rehab programs in one place, allowing you to move through treatment without disruption.

items.title: Residential Inpatient
items.text: 24/7 Structure and Support. Live on-site in a fully structured environment with around-the-clock care. Provides intensive therapy, focused setting to build a strong foundation.

items.title: Partial Hospitalization (PHP)
items.text: Full-Day Treatment. PHP offers full-day care while allowing for more independence. You’ll participate in therapy and recovery work during the day and sleep in your home.

items.title: Intensive Outpatient (IOP)
items.text: Real-Life Integration. Structured therapy multiple days per week while allowing you to maintain work and responsibilities. Focus on applying recovery tools in real-world situations.

items.title: Clinical Aftercare & Alumni
items.text: Long-Term Recovery. Aftercare helps you maintain progress after primary treatment. Relapse prevention strategies, and accountability help you stay on track long-term.

sections.heading: Drug Addiction and Dual Diagnosis Treatment
sections.subheading: Addressing substance use and mental health together for lasting recovery
body: Drug addiction often doesn’t exist on its own. Many people struggling with substance use are also dealing with underlying mental health conditions such as anxiety, depression, trauma, or mood disorders. This is known as dual diagnosis , and treating only the addiction without addressing mental health can make long-term recovery much more difficult.

At Des Moines Wellness Center, dual diagnosis treatment is integrated into every level of care. Our clinical team works to identify and treat both substance use and mental health conditions at the same time, using a combination of therapy and individualized treatment planning. This approach helps reduce relapse risk, improve emotional stability, and support long-term recovery outcomes.

sections.heading: Go to Drug Rehab Without Losing Your Job
sections.subheading: Understanding FMLA and your rights when seeking treatment
body: Many people hesitate to seek treatment because they’re worried about work. In many cases, you can attend drug rehab without losing your job. Under the Family and Medical Leave Act ( FMLA ), eligible employees may take job-protected leave for medical conditions, including substance use disorder treatment. This can allow you to step away from work to focus on recovery and return to your position afterward.

Eligibility depends on factors like your employer and length of employment, but FMLA is designed to protect your job while you get the care you need. At Des Moines Wellness Center, our team can help you understand your options and coordinate treatment in a way that supports both your recovery and your responsibilities.

Located in Des Moines, we serve individuals across Central Iowa who are looking for effective, local drug rehab without the need to travel far from home. Starting treatment can feel overwhelming, but you don’t have to figure it out alone. Our admissions team can help you understand your options, verify your insurance, and guide you toward the right program. Get started today.
